name: Chenrezig
openingText:
འཇིགས་རུང་འཁོར་མཚོར་བྱིང་བའི་འགྲོ་བ་ལ།
Jik rung khor tshor jing wé dro wa la
To beings drowning in saṃsāra's terrifying ocean

དམིགས་མེད་སྙིང་རྗེའི་དབང་གྱུར་སྤྲུལ་པའི་གར།
Mik mé nying jé wang gyur trul pé gar
You manifest as the master of non-referential compassion

བསྟན་མཛད་ལེགས་པའི་ལམ་སྟོན་སྨྲ་བའི་ཉི།
Ten dzé lek pé lam tön ma wé nyi
Guide on the path of excellent teachings, Sun of Speech

བླ་མ་སྤྱན་རས་གཟིགས་ལ་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས།
La ma chen re zik la sol wa deb
Lama Avalokiteśvara, to you I pray

སྐྱེ་བ་ཀུན་ཏུ་མགོན་པོ་སྤྱན་རས་གཟིགས།
Kye wa kün tu gön po chen re zik
In every lifetime, protector Avalokiteśvara

འབྲལ་མེད་གཙུག་གི་རྒྱན་དུ་ཐོབ་པ་དང་།
Dral mé tsuk gi gyen du tob pa dang
May you always remain on my crown without ever separating

དེའི་གསུང་གདམས་དོན་མ་ནོར་ཉམས་བླངས་ནས།
Dé sung dam dön ma nor nyam lang né
Bless me so that, practising the true meaning of your speech correctly

གཏན་བདེའི་རྩ་བ་ཚུགས་པར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས།
Ten dé tsa wa tsuk par jin gyi lob
I may establish the roots of everlasting bliss and happiness
closingText:
དགེ་བ་འདི་ཡིས་མྱུར་དུ་བདག
Gé wa di yi nyur du dag
By this merit, may I swiftly

སྤྱན་རས་གཟིགས་དབང་འགྲུབ་གྱུར་ནས།
Chen re zig wang drub gyur né
Attain the state of Chenrezig

འགྲོ་བ་གཅིག་ཀྱང་མ་ལུས་པ།
Dro wa chik kyang ma lü pa
And may every single living being without exception

དེ་དག་ས་ལ་འགོད་པར་ཤོག།
Dé dak sa la gö par shog
Be established in that very same level.
deityInfo:
CHENREZIG (Avalokiteshvara) - The Bodhisattva of Infinite Compassion

Significance:
Chenrezig embodies the compassion of all Buddhas. He is the patron deity of Tibet and one of the most beloved figures in Buddhism. His name means 'The One Who Looks Down with Unwavering Compassion'.

Key Qualities:
• Infinite compassion for all beings
• The ability to see the suffering of all beings
• Unconditional love and mercy
• Swift response to prayers

The Mantra: OM MANI PADME HUM
This six-syllable mantra contains the entire path to enlightenment:
• OM - Purifies pride/ego
• MA - Purifies jealousy
• NI - Purifies attachment/passion
• PAD - Purifies ignorance/prejudice
• ME - Purifies possessiveness/greed
• HUM - Purifies hatred/aggression

Visualization:
Chenrezig appears white in color with four arms. The first two hands are pressed together at the heart holding a wish-fulfilling jewel. The right hand holds a crystal mala, the left hand holds a white lotus flower. He sits on a moon disc atop a lotus throne.

Benefits of Practice:
• Develops compassion and loving-kindness
• Purifies negative karma
• Brings peace and harmony to the mind
• Protects from harm and obstacles
• Helps fulfill one's spiritual aspirations

Connection to the Dalai Lama:
The Dalai Lama is considered an emanation of Chenrezig, continuing the bodhisattva's work of compassion in the world today.
